assets.purpose: Find the cricothyroid membrane and keep incision planning midline.
assets.prompt: Create a premium, clinically restrained medical illustration for emergency cricothyrotomy membrane identification. Show an adult anterior neck, cropped tightly from just below the jaw to the sternal notch with no face, as a simplified but anatomically plausible schematic that fills most of the frame. From top to bottom on an exactly midline airway column: thyroid cartilage with its laryngeal prominence, the cricothyroid membrane as a clearly highlighted soft depression immediately below it, the cricoid cartilage ring, then two or three tracheal rings fading out. The highlighted membrane is the single teaching focus. Overlay two visually distinct incision paths in warning red-orange: a vertical midline skin incision line centered on the membrane region (do not extend it far down the trachea), and a short horizontal incision line through the membrane itself. Label the vertical line only once as 'Vertical midline skin incision'; do not add a separate 'Midline' label. Balance the labels in two columns: place the three anatomy labels (Thyroid cartilage, Cricothyroid membrane, Cricoid cartilage) down the left and the two incision labels down the right. Use a clean 4:3 educational medical diagram style for an iPhone app: flat vector-like shapes, a light neutral background, subtle anatomy colors with high contrast, and large readable labels aligned in each column with thin leader lines.
assets.avoid: No photorealism, no blood, no scalpel or hands, no patient face, no extra anatomy, no dense textbook layout, no tiny labels, no misspelled or extra text, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Reinforce midline incision discipline by showing the vascular and glandular structures lateral and inferior to the cricothyroid membrane.
assets.prompt: Create a premium, clinically restrained medical illustration of anterior neck danger zones for emergency cricothyrotomy. Show the same simplified anterior neck schematic as a membrane-identification view: thyroid cartilage, cricothyroid membrane, cricoid cartilage, and upper tracheal rings on an exactly midline airway column, cropped tightly from just below the jaw to the sternal notch with no face, so the anatomy fills most of the frame. Shade a narrow vertical midline safe zone over the airway column in a calm blue-green tone. In warning red-orange, show the structures to avoid: paired anterior jugular veins running vertically lateral to the midline on each side, the thyroid isthmus crossing the airway low, over the upper tracheal rings just below the cricoid cartilage (well below the cricothyroid membrane, not up at the membrane), and small laryngeal artery branches coursing near the lateral edges of the membrane. Place each label on the side nearest the structure it names, with its leader line landing exactly on that structure; do not force the labels into even columns or balance the count per side. Use a clean 4:3 educational medical diagram style for an iPhone app: flat vector-like shapes, light neutral background, high contrast, and large readable labels with thin leader lines.
assets.avoid: No photorealism, no blood, no scalpel or hands, no patient face, no dense vascular atlas detail, no tiny labels, no misspelled or extra text, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show safe triangle boundaries and reinforce over-the-rib entry.
assets.prompt: Create a premium, clinically restrained medical illustration for chest tube insertion site selection. Show a simplified lateral chest wall with the safe triangle clearly shaded. Include anterior pectoralis major boundary, posterior latissimus dorsi boundary, 4th-5th intercostal space, and mid-axillary line. Include a small inset showing rib cross-section with the neurovascular bundle along the inferior rib margin and the correct entry above the rib. Use a clean 4:3 medical app diagram style, large labels, high contrast, and no gore.
assets.avoid: No photorealism, no blood, no chest tube inserted into a real patient, no dense textbook plate, no small labels, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show short-axis IJ/carotid relationship, compression check, and needle target.
assets.prompt: Create a premium medical ultrasound teaching illustration for internal jugular central venous catheter placement, drawn as a realistic grayscale B-mode window in short axis (transverse). The ultrasound window is monochrome: black anechoic vessel lumens on gray tissue, with NO saturated red or blue color fill (color would imply Doppler and is wrong here). Show two round vessels: the internal jugular vein positioned LATERAL and slightly SUPERFICIAL (closer to the probe at the top of the window), drawn larger, oval, thin-walled and compressible; and the carotid artery positioned MEDIAL and slightly DEEPER, drawn smaller, round and thick-walled. Include a small probe-orientation indicator at the top showing the probe marker toward the patient's right. Show a needle entering from the skin surface and targeting the internal jugular vein lumen. Optionally include a tiny inset showing the vein compressing while the artery stays round. Use a clean 4:3 iPhone app educational style with large readable labels outside the window and thin leader lines.
assets.avoid: No colorized or Doppler vessels in the B-mode window, no photorealistic neck, no needle in a real patient, no cluttered ultrasound artifacts, no tiny labels, no unrelated anatomy, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show accepted decompression landmarks and the medial danger zone.
assets.prompt: Create a premium, clinically restrained medical illustration for needle decompression landmarks. Show a simplified anterior-lateral chest with two accepted decompression sites: 2nd intercostal space at the midclavicular line and 4th-5th intercostal space at the anterior axillary line. Highlight the lateral site as preferred in larger body habitus. Show an internal mammary artery danger zone close to the sternum and reinforce entering above the rib. Use a 4:3 iPhone medical reference diagram style with large labels and clear color coding.
assets.avoid: No photorealism, no blood, no real patient, no needle visibly puncturing tissue, no dense anatomy plate, no small labels, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Use Tuffier's line to identify safe LP target interspaces below the conus.
assets.prompt: Create a premium, clinically restrained medical illustration for lumbar puncture landmarking. Show a simplified posterior lower back and pelvis with iliac crests, Tuffier's line crossing near L4, and target interspaces L3-L4 and L4-L5. Indicate that targets are below the conus and show a simple midline needle trajectory angled slightly cephalad. Use a clean 4:3 iPhone medical reference style, large readable labels, subtle anatomy colors, and no gore.
assets.avoid: No photorealistic patient, no exposed spinal cord, no blood, no complex textbook anatomy, no tiny labels, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show ultrasound-guided subxiphoid approach and unsafe steep trajectory risk.
assets.prompt: Create a premium medical diagram for ultrasound-guided pericardiocentesis using the subxiphoid approach. Show a simplified torso with a phased-array probe just below the xiphoid and its ultrasound sector showing the heart with a pericardial effusion. Draw BOTH needle trajectories starting from a single entry point at the subxiphoid skin, at the top face of the probe. The correct trajectory is one straight, shallow arrow from that entry running up toward the patient's left shoulder into the effusion. The incorrect trajectory is one straight, overly steep near-vertical arrow from the SAME entry plunging deep toward the heart, crossed out with a red X and labeled 'Steep angle'. Neither arrow loops, curls, or points downward out of the body. Use a 4:3 iPhone app educational style with large labels, no gore, and restrained clinical colors.
assets.avoid: No photorealism, no graphic needle penetration, no blood, no cluttered ECG or monitor content, no small labels, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show inferior crus target and globe-safe cut direction.
assets.prompt: Create a premium, clinically restrained medical illustration for lateral canthotomy and inferior cantholysis, drawn as a FRONTAL (anterior) view of a single eye as seen looking at the patient's face — NOT a side-on or sagittal cross-section of the globe. Show the open eye with upper and lower eyelids, the iris and pupil, and the lateral canthus at the outer corner where the lids meet. Show the horizontal canthotomy incision extending laterally from the lateral canthus toward the lateral orbital rim. Show the inferior crus as a short band at the lower outer corner, deep to the lid, marked as the release target and labeled simply 'Inferior crus'. Show a pair of scissors and a cut-direction arrow at the lateral canthus pointing DOWNWARD AND OUTWARD toward the outer corner and the lateral orbital rim — that is, away from the eyeball, with the globe staying to the inner side of the cut. The arrow must never point toward or across the globe. The 'Lateral canthus' label and its leader line must land on the OUTER corner of the eye where the lids meet and the incision begins — never on the inner (medial, nose-side) corner. Keep the globe intact and centered. Use a clean 4:3 iPhone medical reference style, flat vector-like shapes, large readable labels outside the anatomy with thin leader lines, no gore, and minimal color coding with red-orange only for the incision and cut direction.
assets.avoid: No sagittal or side cross-section of the eye, no lens/anterior-chamber cutaway view, no photorealistic injured eye, no blood, no graphic scissors entering tissue, no frightening image, no dense anatomy plate, no tiny labels, no duplicated or extra text, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show LLQ entry site and inferior epigastric vessel avoidance.
assets.prompt: Create a premium medical landmark diagram for paracentesis. Show a simplified abdomen with umbilicus, ASIS, LLQ entry site, inferior epigastric vessel course, and underlying bowel loops. Place the LLQ entry site clearly lateral to the midline and away from both the bowel loops and the inferior epigastric vessels, so avoidance is shown by position rather than words. Make the safe LLQ target visually clear. Use a clean 4:3 iPhone medical reference style with large readable labels and restrained colors.
assets.avoid: No photorealistic abdomen, no needle in real skin, no blood, no crowded anatomy, no tiny labels, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show diaphragm boundary, posterior-lateral entry site, and over-the-rib rule.
assets.prompt: Create a premium medical landmark diagram for thoracentesis site selection. Show a simplified posterior-lateral chest wall with pleural fluid above the diaphragm, a safe entry site above the rib, and the neurovascular bundle at the inferior rib margin. Include a clear diaphragm boundary to prevent too-low entry. Use a clean 4:3 iPhone medical reference style with large labels and restrained colors.
assets.avoid: No photorealism, no real patient puncture, no blood, no dense ultrasound artifacts, no tiny labels, no decorative background, no watermark.

assets.purpose: Show needle tip visualization rather than shaft-only false reassurance.
assets.prompt: Create a premium ultrasound teaching diagram for ultrasound-guided peripheral IV placement. Show a short-axis ultrasound view with a compressible vein, needle shaft, and needle tip as a bright dot entering the vein lumen. Emphasize anterior wall tenting and the warning not to mistake shaft visualization for true tip location. Use a clean 4:3 iPhone medical reference style with large readable labels and dark ultrasound panel.
assets.avoid: No photorealistic arm, no real puncture, no blood, no cluttered ultrasound artifacts, no tiny labels, no decorative background, no watermark.
